content.text:
Repro fails for exactly the reported reason: the picker gets archived users it didn't request. Now the minimal fix at the cause.

**Mechanism:** the cache key is `users:${opts.team}`, which ignores `includeArchived`, so an archived-inclusive fetch for a team is served back to a later picker request for that same team; adding `includeArchived` to the key makes the two requests distinct cache entries.

content.text:
Fixed. Both tests pass.

**The bug:** In `src/userService.js`, `getUsers` built its cache key from `opts.team` only — `users:${opts.team}`. The `includeArchived` flag was dropped from the key, so an archived-inclusive fetch and an archived-excluding fetch for the same team shared one cache entry.

**Why the symptom matched the report:** When some other view (e.g. admin) fetched a team with `includeArchived: true`, that archived-inclusive list was cached under `users:<team>`. The people picker then requested the same team with `includeArchived: false`, got a cache hit, and received the archived users it never asked for. Since the cache TTL is 60s, refreshing after a minute expired the entry and cleared it — exactly "refreshing after a minute clears it."

**The fix:** Include the flag in the key: `users:${opts.team}:${opts.includeArchived ? "all" : "active"}`, so the two request shapes map to distinct cache entries.

I kept the repro test (`test/userService.test.js`) as the regression guard, and left `cache.js` and everything else untouched since the cause was solely the key construction.

result:
Fixed. Both tests pass.

**The bug:** In `src/userService.js`, `getUsers` built its cache key from `opts.team` only — `users:${opts.team}`. The `includeArchived` flag was dropped from the key, so an archived-inclusive fetch and an archived-excluding fetch for the same team shared one cache entry.

**Why the symptom matched the report:** When some other view (e.g. admin) fetched a team with `includeArchived: true`, that archived-inclusive list was cached under `users:<team>`. The people picker then requested the same team with `includeArchived: false`, got a cache hit, and received the archived users it never asked for. Since the cache TTL is 60s, refreshing after a minute expired the entry and cleared it — exactly "refreshing after a minute clears it."

**The fix:** Include the flag in the key: `users:${opts.team}:${opts.includeArchived ? "all" : "active"}`, so the two request shapes map to distinct cache entries.

I kept the repro test (`test/userService.test.js`) as the regression guard, and left `cache.js` and everything else untouched since the cause was solely the key construction.
